questions.question: Analyse the concept of public facilities and explain their importance in society.
questions.answer: Public facilities are essential services provided mainly by the government for the welfare of citizens. They improve health, education, transport and living conditions in society.
answer_outline: Examples such as water supply, schools and hospitals

questions.question: Explain why clean drinking water is considered an essential public facility.
questions.answer: Clean drinking water is essential because it supports health, prevents diseases and is necessary for daily life and survival.

questions.question: Explain the role of government in providing public facilities.
questions.answer: The government is responsible for ensuring that essential services such as water, healthcare, education and transport are available to all citizens.

questions.question: Discuss the relationship between taxes and public facilities.
questions.answer: Taxes collected by the government are used to build and maintain public facilities such as roads, hospitals and water systems.

questions.question: Evaluate the importance of public healthcare facilities.
questions.answer: Public healthcare facilities provide affordable medical services, reduce disease spread and improve overall health of the population.

questions.question: Explain how public education facilities contribute to national development.
questions.answer: Public schools provide education to citizens, helping develop skills, knowledge and human resources necessary for national development.

questions.question: Explain the difference between public and private facilities with examples.
questions.answer: Public facilities are provided by the government for everyone, while private facilities are run by private organisations and usually require payment.

questions.question: Analyse how lack of public facilities affects communities.
questions.answer: Lack of public facilities leads to poor health, low education levels and reduced economic opportunities.

questions.question: Explain the importance of equal access to public facilities.
questions.answer: Equal access ensures fairness and allows all citizens to benefit from essential services regardless of income or location.

questions.question: Explain how transportation facilities contribute to economic development.
questions.answer: Transportation allows movement of people and goods, supporting trade, industry and economic growth.

questions.question: Explain the role of sanitation facilities in public health.
questions.answer: Sanitation facilities maintain hygiene and reduce the spread of infectious diseases.

questions.question: Analyse how electricity supports modern infrastructure.
questions.answer: Electricity powers homes, industries, schools and hospitals, making it essential for development.

questions.question: Explain how inequality in public facilities affects social development.
questions.answer: Unequal access to facilities creates gaps in education, health and economic opportunities.

questions.question: Explain why governments must ensure fair distribution of public facilities.
questions.answer: Fair distribution ensures that all citizens benefit from essential services and promotes social justice.

questions.question: Explain how public facilities support community development.
questions.answer: Facilities such as schools, hospitals and roads help improve infrastructure and living standards.

questions.question: Explain how citizens contribute to maintaining public facilities.
questions.answer: Citizens contribute by paying taxes, using facilities responsibly and protecting public property.

questions.question: Analyse how strong public infrastructure promotes national progress.
questions.answer: Strong infrastructure improves productivity, supports industries and enhances quality of life.

questions.question: Explain how public facilities promote social welfare.
questions.answer: Public facilities improve health, education and safety, leading to better living conditions.

questions.question: Explain how responsible governance improves public services.
questions.answer: Responsible governance ensures efficient management of resources and fair distribution of facilities.

questions.question: Explain how public facilities support sustainable development.
questions.answer: Public facilities support sustainable development by improving infrastructure while ensuring long-term welfare of society.

questions.question: Evaluate the importance of government investment in public facilities.
questions.answer: Government investment helps build infrastructure, improve services and support economic growth.

questions.question: Explain how public facilities contribute to equality and social justice.
questions.answer: Public facilities ensure that everyone has access to essential services regardless of income or background.

questions.question: Analyse the role of infrastructure in improving quality of life.
questions.answer: Infrastructure such as roads, water supply and electricity improves daily living conditions and economic opportunities.

questions.question: Explain why strong public facilities are essential for modern societies.
questions.answer: Modern societies depend on services such as water supply, electricity and transport for development and daily life.

questions.question: Evaluate the overall importance of public facilities in national development.
questions.answer: Public facilities improve infrastructure, support economic growth and enhance the well-being of citizens.
